api/v3/handlers/customers.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for core customer CRUD (list, create, get, delete, upsert) and the entry point wired by api/v3/server. Sub-packages (billing/, charges/, credits/, entitlementaccess/) own customer-scoped resource slices with distinct service dependencies.

patterns.name: Public Handler interface + unexported handler struct
patterns.description: Declare a public Handler interface listing every operation method returning a typed handler alias; back it with an unexported handler struct. Expose a New() constructor.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { ListCustomers() ListCustomersHandler; CreateCustomer() CreateCustomerHandler; ... }; type handler struct { resolveNamespace func(ctx context.Context) (string, error); service customer.Service; options []httptransport.HandlerOption }

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param operations
patterns.description: Operations with path parameters (customerID) use NewHandlerWithArgs[Req, Resp, Params]; plain POST/LIST operations use NewHandler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via injected resolver, never from request params
patterns.description: Always call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) in the decoder; never extract namespace from URL or query params directly.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Per-operation type aliases before method body
patterns.description: Each operation file defines package-level type aliases XxxRequest, XxxResponse, XxxParams, XxxHandler before the method body for discoverability and compile-time verification.
patterns.example: type ( CreateCustomerRequest = customer.CreateCustomerInput; CreateCustomerResponse = api.BillingCustomer; CreateCustomerHandler httptransport.Handler[CreateCustomerRequest, CreateCustomerResponse] )

patterns.name: Goverter for API-to-domain struct conversion
patterns.description: Declare conversion function vars with goverter annotations in convert.go; regenerate convert.gen.go with go generate. The generated file carries //go:build !goverter and must never be hand-edited.

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder always appended last
patterns.description: Every httptransport.AppendOptions call must include httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) so domain errors map to correct HTTP status codes. Sub-package error encoders are prepended before it.

patterns.name: Deleted-customer guard before mutations
patterns.description: Any operation mutating an existing customer must fetch it first and return apierrors.NewGoneError when cus.IsDeleted() is true.
patterns.example: cus, err := h.service.GetCustomer(ctx, ...); if cus.IsDeleted() { return ..., apierrors.NewGoneError(ctx, errors.New("customer is deleted")) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Handler interface and New() constructor; lists every operation the package exposes. Add new operation methods here first.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing an operation in the Handler interface causes a compile error when api/v3/server tries to call it.

key_file_guides.role: Goverter annotation source for API-to-domain struct conversions. Edit annotations here and run make generate; never touch convert.gen.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting //go:generate header or goverter:variables block breaks regeneration silently.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Goverter — DO NOT EDIT. Has !goverter build tag so it is excluded when goverter itself runs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hand-editing this file causes drift that will be overwritten on the next make generate.

key_file_guides.role: ListCustomers handler with pagination (page number/size defaulting to 1/20), sort parsing via request.ParseSortBy, and filter parsing via filters.FromAPIFilterString.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dropping page.Validate() silently accepts invalid page sizes; omitting filter fields produces incomplete filtering.

key_file_guides.role: UpsertCustomer handler; performs a pre-fetch to preserve the immutable Key field before calling service.UpdateCustomer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If the pre-fetch and key-backfill is removed, service.UpdateCustomer will wipe the customer key in the DB.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package handler for customer-scoped billing profile overrides and Stripe session endpoints.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Stripe session endpoints (checkout, portal) share the same handler; billing profile must be resolved before calling Stripe app.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package handler gated by credits.enabled; injected services may be noops when credits are off.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not assume ledger or accountResolver are real implementations when credits.enabled=false.

key_file_guides.role: Lists flat-fee and usage-based charges; explicitly excludes meta.ChargeTypeCreditPurchase.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Including credit-purchase charges here exposes them on the wrong API surface.

anti_patterns: Extracting namespace from HTTP request params instead of calling h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Hand-editing convert.gen.go instead of updating convert.go annotations and running make generate
Omitting apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() from httptransport.AppendOptions — domain errors will not map to correct HTTP status codes
Mutating an existing customer without first fetching it and checking IsDeleted()
Including meta.ChargeTypeCreditPurchase in the charges list endpoint's ChargeTypes filter

decisions.decision: One operation per file (create.go, delete.go, get.go, list.go, upsert.go)
decisions.rationale: Keeps each handler independently reviewable and prevents a single file growing unbounded as new operations are added.

decisions.decision: Goverter-generated struct mapping instead of hand-written conversion helpers
decisions.rationale: Compile-time checked field mapping eliminates silent drift between API types and domain types as either evolves.

decisions.decision: Sub-packages (billing/, credits/, charges/, entitlementaccess/) own their operation slices
decisions.rationale: Customer-scoped resources have distinct service dependencies and error-encoding needs; co-locating them in the parent handler.go would couple unrelated services and error encoders.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new customer operation (e.g. ArchiveCustomer) following package conventions
